zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward de Hillary於大約1319年出生在英國英格蘭德比夏爾桑迪亞可時，他的父親Henry Hillary41歲，母親Joane II De Marmion36歲。他和Alice Grey至少有1個兒子。他去世於1362年6月享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward de Hillary于大约1319年出生在英国英格兰德比夏爾桑迪亞可时，他的父亲Henry Hillary41岁，母亲Joane II De Marmion36岁。他和Alice Grey至少有1个儿子。他去世于1362年6月享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hillary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran.
English (of Norman origin):

from the Middle English and Old French male or female personal name Hilari(e), Ilari(e), and Elari(e), from Latin Hilarius and Hilaria ‘joyful’ (from Greek hilaros). The Latin name was chosen by many early Christians to express their joy and hope of salvation, and was borne by several saints, including a 4th-century bishop of Poitiers (died 368) noted for his vigorous resistance to the Arian heresy, and a 5th-century bishop of Arles. Largely due to veneration of Saint Hilarius of Poitiers, the name became popular in France in the forms Hilari and Hilaire, and was brought to England by the Norman conquerors. Although quite popular in France, it was only occasionally used in England, where it was confused with the name in 2 below.

variants.name: Ellery
meanings: variant of   Hillary  .
habitational name from Elleray in Windermere (Westmorland), probably from Old Norse elri ‘alder’ + vrá ‘nook’.
English:

variants.name: Gloor
meanings: Swiss German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Hilarius (see Hillary ).

variants.name: Hillier
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Surrey): occupational name for a roofer from Middle English hil(l)ier, hul(li)er, hel(li)er ‘roofer, slater, tiler’, agent nouns from the Middle English verb hil(i)en, hel(i)an, hul(i)en ‘to put a roof on; cover with a roof’.
French: from the personal name Hillier, a vernacular form of Hilaire (see Hillary ).

variants.name: Alario
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Orazio, Carlo, Rocco, Salvatore, Vita, Vitina.
Italian: from a vernacular form of the personal name Ilario, from Latin Hilarius (see Hillary ).
Italian (Sicily): probably a status name from dialect alariu ‘herald’.

variants.name: Glor
meanings: German: from a vernacular form of the Latin personal name Hilarius (see Hillary ).

variants.name: Hillery
meanings: English: variant of   Hillary  . This name has long been established in Ireland (Clare).

variants.name: Hilaire
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Pierre, Antoine, Chantal, Edeline, Georges, Luc, Maryse, Mimose, Serge, Yolaine.
French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the personal name Hilaire, French form of Hillary 1. This surname is most common in Haiti. Compare Helaire .

variants.name: Hilleary
meanings: English (Essex): variant of   Hillary  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Allaire
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Andre, Lucien, Alphonse, Antoine, Armand, Emile, Michel, Pierre, Raoul, Raymonde, Rolande.
French: from the Old French personal name Allaire, a variant of Hilaire (see Hillary ). Compare Dallaire .

History: Jean and Charles Allaire, two brothers from the Poitou region of France, are recorded in Quebec City, QC, in 1662 and 1663, respectively, the years of their marriage. The descendants of one or another brother also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Dallaire, Delair, and Delaire. — Alexandre Allaire, a Huguenot refugee, brought the name from La Rochelle in France to New Rochelle, NY, in 1686, via the West Indies. He is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Hilario
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Julio, Alfredo, Luis, Abelardo, Arturo, Eduardo, Francisco, Juan, Mario, Pedro, Carlos, Cesar.  Portuguese Afonso, Joao.
Portuguese (Hilário) and Spanish: from a personal name ofthe same origin as Hillary 1.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard

variants.name: Edvard

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Edward de Hillary was born about 1319, in Sandiacre, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Hillary, was 41 and his mother, Joane II De Marmion, was 36. He had at least 1 son with Alice Grey. He died in June 1362, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward de Hillary est né environ 1319, à Sandiacre, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry Hillary, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Joane II De Marmion, avait 36 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Alice Grey. Il est décédé en juin 1362, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward de Hillary um 1319 in Sandiacre, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Hillary 41 und seine Mutter Joane II De Marmion 36. Er hatte mit Alice Grey mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb im Juni 1362 im Alter von 44 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward de Hillary è nato il circa 1319 nel Sandiacre, Derbyshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Henry Hillary, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Joane II De Marmion, aveva 36 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Alice Grey. Morì nel giugno 1362, all'età di 44 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Edward de Hillary(이)가 대략 1319년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 샌디아크르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Hillary(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Joane II De Marmion(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Alice Grey(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1362년 6월 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward de Hillary nasceu aproximadamente 1319, em Sandiacre, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry Hillary, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Joane II De Marmion, tinha 36 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Alice Grey. Ele faleceu em junho de 1362, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward de Hillary родился около 1319 г. в местности сэндякр, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry Hillary было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Joane II De Marmion, 36. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Alice Grey. Он умер июнь 1362 г. в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward de Hillary nació aproximadamente 1319, en Sandiacre, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry Hillary, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Joane II De Marmion, tenía 36 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Alice Grey. Murió en junio de 1362, a la edad de 44 años.
